steps.title: Identify the subject in the relative clause
steps.content: In “which ______ located in Newark, New Jersey,” the relative pronoun “which” is the subject of the clause. This pronoun refers to “Newark Liberty International Airport,” a singular noun.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: A subject and its verb must agree in number. “Is” is the singular form that agrees with “which.” “Are,” “were,” and “have been” are all plural forms that violate subject-verb agreement. That matches choice A — A is correct.
